technical_documents.description: An ultra-low-cost, off-grid agro-technical blueprint that converts upcycled organic shipping pallets into a decentralized vertical sprouting infrastructure. Utilizing passive gravity drainage and manual micro-misting, this machine accelerates grain germination into high-nutrient green forage mats, bypassing high-cost automated systems entirely to optimize livestock feed self-sufficiency.
technical_documents.problem_statement: Smallholder dairy farmers in semi-arid regions expend 55–70% of gross agricultural revenues on commercial concentrate livestock feeds. While 1 kg of raw barley grain can sprout into 6 kg of nutrient-dense green fodder mats, existing automated commercial hydroponic enclosures cost over $2,000, creating an inaccessible capital barrier for off-grid family farms.
technical_documents.engineering_solution: The design re-engineers five recycled wooden pallets into a 5-tier modular A-frame vertical growth rack lined with food-grade high-density polyethylene (HDPE) germination trays. Lacking automated solenoid timers or grid-dependent pumps, the system operates via a manual hand-pumped micro-misting wand applied 3x daily. This method controls root-zone saturation without stagnant water accumulation, turning 5 kg of raw barley seed into 30 kg of highly metabolic, enzyme-rich fresh green forage daily within a ultra-compact 2 square meter footprint.

bill_of_materials.name: High-Pressure Manual Hand-Pump Misting Wand Assembly
High-Germination Barley Seed (25 kg Starter Stock)

specifications.dimensions: 5-tier A-frame rack within a 2 sqm footprint
specifications.production_yield: 30 kg of fresh green forage fodder mats every 6-day cycle

step_by_step_build: Step 1: Dismantle pallets; cut planks to build 5-tier A-frame rack with 25cm vertical spacing between shelves.
Step 2: Line each shelf with HDPE trays; drill 3mm drainage holes every 10cm for root aeration.
Step 3: Soak barley seed in water for 8 hours; spread evenly at 1 kg per tray (1cm depth).
Step 4: Mist trays 3x daily (morning, noon, evening) using the hand-pump wand; maintain shade net covering.
Step 5: Harvest on day 6 by lifting the entire root mat; feed directly to livestock. Start new batch daily for continuous supply.

comments.text: Cut our dairy feed costs from 180 to 72 XLM/month for 3 cows. Cows love the fresh fodder.
Using this with wheat grain too — works great. Mould issues if you overcrowd the trays.
